Their scripts are visible on your DDD dashboard but are never eligible for IC. We recommend filtering your dashboard view by Credit-flagged HCPs only when doing self-calculations. One legitimate correction was found: the 340B retroactive lookback exceeded the 60-day policy window. A partial IC correction of $2,800 has been requested and will be processed before the payment run. A full variance bridge document has been sent to you and your District Manager.

inquiries.title: Specialty Pharmacy Not Mapped to Territory - Creditable Scripts Not Generating IC Credit
inquiries.field_rep_says: "One of my HIV specialists told me that almost all his patients now fill through Harbor Specialty Pharmacy. But none of those scripts seem to show up in my dashboard. My numbers dropped significantly this quarter even though his prescribing hasn't changed at all."
inquiries.what_happened: Dr. Steven Patel (NPI 1934567281) recently began routing HIV prescriptions through Harbor Specialty Pharmacy, a pharmacy that distributes through a new regional distributor channel. This channel is not yet registered in the 867 ship-to territory mapping table. As a result, Dr. Patel's scripts are appearing correctly in the distributor sales feed but are not being attributed to any territory. They are effectively 'unassigned' transactions in the 867 data - visible in the raw feed but not flowing into any rep's IC credit calculation. The rep is seeing a decline in their dashboard numbers that does not reflect any actual change in Dr. Patel's prescribing behavior. The prescribing is real, the scripts are being filled, but the configuration gap in the mapping table is causing a complete credit blackout for this pharmacy channel. Specialty crediting context: Dr. Patel is an Infectious Disease specialist (code 44), a fully creditable specialty for all Gilead HIV treatment products. His Credit flag is correctly active. This is not a specialty or credit eligibility issue - every one of his scripts is creditable. The problem is purely a 867 distributor feed configuration gap that is preventing correctly creditable scripts from being assigned to the rep's territory.
steward_steps: Pull 867 distributor data for Dr. Patel's NPI (1934567281) over the past 90 days: identify all Ship-To IDs receiving his Biktarvy prescriptions and flag any that are new or unrecognized.
Identify the Ship-To ID associated with Harbor Specialty Pharmacy and check the 867 ship-to territory mapping table: confirm whether this Ship-To ID is mapped to any territory.
Confirm Dr. Patel's Credit flag status in the sales crediting flag file: verify specialty code 44 (Infectious Disease) is active and the Credit flag is correctly set for the rep's territory.

Pull IQVIA DDD prescribing data for NPI 1934567281 and compare to 867 transaction volume for the same period: quantify the gap between prescriptions written (DDD) and transactions credited (867) to estimate the total missing IC volume.
Verify whether other HCPs in the rep's territory are also routing through Harbor Specialty Pharmacy - if so, the missing volume may be broader than a single prescriber.
Submit a mapping DCR to Sales Ops to link Harbor Specialty Pharmacy's Ship-To ID to the rep's territory in the 867 configuration table.
Submit a retroactive IC correction request for all unassigned transactions from the affected period, with the 867 raw transaction data and DDD comparison as supporting documentation.
inquiries.resolution_and_response_to_rep: We confirmed Dr. Patel recently switched the majority of his HIV patients to Harbor Specialty Pharmacy. This pharmacy's distributor Ship-To ID was not yet registered in the 867 ship-to territory mapping table, which caused all transactions through this channel to appear as unassigned - generating no IC credit for your territory. Dr. Patel is an Infectious Disease specialist (code 44) with an active Credit flag for all Gilead HIV treatment products. Every one of the missing scripts is fully creditable - this is a configuration gap, not a credit eligibility issue. DCR #DCR-2024-1351 has been submitted to add Harbor Specialty Pharmacy's Ship-To ID to your territory's mapping configuration. Once processed, all future transactions through this channel will credit correctly. We cross-referenced DDD vs. 867 data to quantify the gap: estimated missing volume for the affected period is 22 units of Biktarvy across the quarter. A retroactive IC correction request has been submitted for the full unassigned transaction volume. Corrected credits will be reflected in the next IC processing cycle. We also checked whether other HCPs in your territory use Harbor Specialty Pharmacy - two additional creditable prescribers were identified. Their unassigned volume has been included in the same retroactive correction request.

inquiries.title: High-Volume HIV Prescriber Showing as Low Priority Due to Incomplete Data Capture
inquiries.field_rep_says: "My CRM has Dr. David Coleman at Tier 3 - low priority - and he's not even on my required call list. But I've been in his office and I can tell you he is one of the busiest HIV physicians in the county. He runs a dedicated infectious disease practice and I know firsthand he's writing Biktarvy constantly. Why is the system treating him like he barely matters?"
inquiries.what_happened: Dr. David Coleman (NPI 1639274850) operates an HIV specialty clinic and is a high-volume prescriber. His CRM tier is Tier 3 because the segmentation model is underestimating his prescribing volume. He dispenses exclusively through a restricted specialty pharmacy network (SHPN) that has a limited data-sharing arrangement with IQVIA - approximately 60% of his actual Rx volume is captured in DDD, with the remaining 40% suppressed due to SHPN's data contribution restrictions. The segmentation model assigned his tier based on the incomplete DDD pull. No DCR was submitted to manually override his tier. Specialty crediting context: Dr. Coleman is an Infectious Disease specialist (code 44), a fully creditable specialty for all Gilead HIV treatment products. His Credit flag is correctly set and active. This means every script he writes that does appear in the 867 and DDD data is generating sales credit for the rep - the problem is that the incomplete data capture is understating his actual contribution to the rep's IC attainment, and keeping him at Tier 3 means the rep is not engaging him with the call frequency appropriate for what is likely the highest-volume creditable prescriber in the territory. There is no specialty or credit eligibility issue here - this is purely a data completeness problem affecting targeting.
steward_steps: Pull DDD data for NPI 1639274850: extract available Gilead HIV Rx volume for 6 and 12 months.
Check the IQVIA pharmacy coverage map for SHPN: confirm the known data capture rate for this network (approximately 60%).
Apply a data gap adjustment: gross up the DDD-reported volume by the inverse of the capture rate to estimate true prescribing potential.
Cross-reference with IQVIA XPO projected data: XPO uses a broader pharmacy capture methodology and may provide a more complete picture.

Confirm Dr. Coleman's specialty in IQVIA OneKey (Infectious Disease, code 44) and verify his Credit flag is active in the sales crediting flag file for all applicable Gilead HIV products in the rep's portfolio.
Submit a DCR for a manual Tier 1 override, with the adjusted DDD volume estimate and XPO cross-reference as supporting data.

Escalate the SHPN data gap issue to the segmentation team: other creditable HCPs in this territory who dispense through SHPN may be similarly under-tiered, meaning the rep is under-engaging multiple creditable prescribers.
inquiries.resolution_and_response_to_rep: We confirmed Dr. Coleman's Tier 3 assignment is caused by incomplete data capture. His primary dispensing pharmacy (SHPN) suppresses approximately 40% of his Rx volume from the DDD feed used in segmentation. After applying a data gap adjustment and cross-referencing IQVIA XPO, his estimated true Gilead HIV Rx volume places him in the top 10% of prescribers in your territory - consistent with what you have observed in the field. Importantly, Dr. Coleman is an Infectious Disease specialist (code 44) with an active Credit flag for all Gilead HIV treatment products. There is no specialty or credit eligibility issue here. Every script he writes is creditable - the segmentation model has simply been working with incomplete data, which caused the system to under-prioritize a prescriber who should be one of your most frequently called targets. DCR #DCR-2024-1332 has been submitted to manually override his tier to Tier 1. Your call plan will update within 24 hours. We have also escalated the SHPN data gap issue to the segmentation team to audit all creditable specialty HCPs in your territory who dispense primarily through SHPN for similar under-tiering.
